hotspots.title: PP-Vox ‘national priority’ pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Spain's conservative PP and far-right Vox have formed coalition agreements in Extremadura and Aragón, implementing 'national priority' policies that prioritize Spanish citizens for public services and aid. These pacts have sparked nationwide controversy and legal challenges.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its polarizing nature, challenging constitutional principles of equality and sparking moral outrage from opposition parties and human rights groups.

hotspots.title: Tisza Party’s cabinet formation in Hungary
hotspots.blurb: Hungary's new Tisza Party, led by Péter Magyar, is forming a government after a landslide victory, appointing ministers and negotiating with the EU to unlock frozen funds. The party promises systemic reforms and transparency but faces challenges from entrenched interests.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its high-stakes political transition, potential EU conflicts, and the emotional framing of ending Orbán's authoritarian rule.

hotspots.title: Pete Hegseth’s Pentagon purges and Iran tensions
hotspots.blurb: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has overseen a series of high-profile firings at the Pentagon, including the Army's top chaplain and Navy Secretary, amid escalating tensions with Iran. The US maintains a naval blockade of Iran and has deployed additional aircraft carriers to the region.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its high-stakes geopolitical implications, moral outrage over military leadership instability, and the affective framing of a potential global conflict.

hotspots.title: AfD’s rise and democratic backlash in Germany
hotspots.blurb: Germany's far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) has surged in polls, leading in some eastern states and facing internal conflicts over extremism allegations. The party's rise has sparked protests, legal challenges, and debates over Germany's democratic resilience.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its existential implications for German democracy, the moral outrage over extremism, and the polarized contention between the AfD and mainstream parties.

hotspots.title: Italy’s Liberation Day and far-right tensions
hotspots.blurb: Italy's April 25 Liberation Day celebrations were marked by tensions between the far-right government and left-wing protesters. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni emphasized national unity, while protests targeted her government's policies and alleged fascist sympathies.
hotspots.affect: This commands disproportionate attention due to its symbolic resonance with Italy's anti-fascist history, the moral outrage over far-right normalization, and the affective framing of democratic values at stake.
